channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Gbenga Hashim sues Accord, INEC, urges court to uphold him as party’s presidential candidate
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ibrahim Hassan-WuyoThe Federal High Court in Abuja has adjourned to July 14, 2026, a suit filed by Accord Party presidential aspirant, Dr. Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim, seeking to be recognised as the party’s presidential candidate for the 2027 general election. The suit came up on Thursday amid t

item.title: TeKnowledge Inks Deal with Equinix to Fast-Track Hybrid, Multi-Cloud Adoption in West Africa
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: TeKnowledge, a technology services company, and Equinix, a digital infrastructure company, have announced a partnership to support enterprises and public sector organizations across West Africa to accelerate secure hybrid and multi-cloud adoption and enable AI-ready digital infrastructure across Wes

item.title: France urges calm among fans during World Cup game against Morocco
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Several members of Morocco's national team this year were born in France and play for French clubs. The post France urges calm among fans during World Cup game against Morocco appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Business Owners Now Sell Kuli-Kuli Since Tinubu Became President –Obi
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has said that many Nigerians who owned thriving businesses before President Bola Tinubu assumed office have lost their businesses and are now selling kuli-kuli to survive, insisting that the country has experienced worse

item.title: ICE agents are making house calls for online critics
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: A few hours after checking into a hotel in New York City, David Streever woke up to a call from the front desk saying someone was looking for him. Streever had just landed on a return trip from Finland, where he'd vacationed with his daughter. Though Streever didn't know it yet, while he'd been away

item.title: Corruption Trial: Court remands ex-CCT chairman in prison
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: It was alleged that in 2021 he used his wife’s bank account to collect N5.5 million from a contractor engaged to paint the headquarters of the CCT. An Abuja High Court on Thursday remanded the former Chairman of the Code of Conduct Tribunal (CCT), Danladi Umar, in the Kuje Medium Security Custodial

item.title: ACAEBIN Strengthens Nigeria’s Open Banking Revolution Int’l Study Tour
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: In a bold move to fast-track Nigeria’s Open Banking future, the Association of Chief Audit Executives of Banks in Nigeria (ACAEBIN) has wrapped up a high-powered International Training and Study Tour in partnership with Euromoney and the Association of Banks in Singapore (ABS). Themed “Open Ba

item.title: Taraba govt begins fresh civil service recruitment
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Taraba State Government on Thursday announced the commencement of another set of employment into the civil service. Mr. Philip Kundila, Chairman of the Taraba State Civil Service Commission, disclosed at a press briefing that a total of 1,805 applicants are targeted for employment during the exe

item.title: APM Challenges Tinubu To Account For Subsidy Spending

item.description: The Allied People’s Movement (APM) has called on President Bola Tinubu’s All Progressives Congress (APC) administration to provide Nigerians with a comprehensive account of the trillions of naira realised from the removal of the fuel subsidy since May 2023, insisting that the government must explain

item.title: APM Asks APC to Account for Subsidy Removal Proceeds
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Allied People’s Movement (APM) has called on the President Bola Tinubu-led All Progressives Congress (APC) administration to provide Nigerians with a comprehensive account of the proceeds realized from the removal of fuel subsidy since May 2023, arguing that citizens deserve transparency

item.title: Insecurity: We Won’t Compromise On Students’ Safety – Idris
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Kebbi State Government has reaffirmed that the closure of schools in parts of the state affected by banditry remains a necessary measure to protect the lives of students and teachers. The government noted that the decision, taken about seven months ago, was not political but a direct response to

item.title: Shell Recognised For Landmark Achievements At NOG Energy Awards
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Shell has won awards at the 25th edition of the Nigeria Oil and Gas (NOG) Energy Week Conference and Exhibition in Abuja in recognition of pioneering contributions to the energy industry in the country. It won the Equality in Energy award while a Shell company, Daystar Power was honoured with the Su

item.title: Zulum urges corpers to ‘feel at home’ in Borno, announces N50,000 gifts to each corp member
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Borno Governor Babagana Zulum urged NYSC members to feel at home and announced N50,000 gifts while promising youth support. The post Zulum urges corpers to ‘feel at home’ in Borno, announces N50,000 gifts to each corp member appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Completesports

According to Turkish outlet Takagazete, Trabzonspor and Wolves are in the final stages of negotiations, with the Premier League club insisting that

item.title: Schwab strategists warn of a major market shift: The era of easy index gains is officially over
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: More geopolitical instability, more frequent supply shocks and macro volatility are facing investors in an era that’s just starting, says Charles Schwab.

item.description: Okpala said the faction remained united and hopeful that the apex court would deliver justice. The post LP crisis threatens 2027 preparations — Chieftain appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Europe Faces Fresh Volatility After Iran Flare-Up, Officials Say
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Europe’s economy is looking at a fresh bout of uncertainty following Donald Trump’s declaration that the US ceasefire with Iran is over, officials from the region said.

item.title: Michael Jackson biopic crosses N800 million at Nigerian box office
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Michael Jackson biopic Michael has crossed the N800 million mark at the Nigerian box office, becoming one of the highest-grossing films ever released in the country despite not being screened by Filmhouse Group, one of West Africa's largest cinema exhibitors. The post Michael Jackson biopic cros

item.title: Alleged $4.5bn fraud: Court admits Emefiele’s statements as exhibits
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: An Ikeja Special Offences Court on Thursday admitted as evidence extra-judicial statements made by former Central Bank of Nigeria, CBN, Governor Godwin Emefiele in his trial for alleged abuse of office. Justice Rahman Oshodi upheld the statements as exhibits, allowing the substantive trial

item.title: Cardi B, S’Eagles Star Maduka Okoye Turn Heads At Paris Fashion Week
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: American rapper Cardi B and Super Eagles goalkeeper Maduka Okoye drew attention after making a joint appearance at Paris Fashion Week on Wednesday. The duo attended the Jean Paul Gaultier Fall/Winter 2026 Haute Couture Show, where Okoye was seen escorting Cardi B to her seat before the pair watched

item.title: Iran Rushes Out Oil Exports as Tensions With US Flare Again
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Iran has rushed out oil tankers carrying roughly 11 million barrels of crude in the past 24 hours as tensions flare again with the US and President Donald Trump threatens to reimpose a blockade on its ports.

item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: Nigerians in the U.S. seeking visas to travel to Nigeria must now apply directly at Nigerian diplomatic missions.…

item.title: Disu Recommits to Global Policing Cooperation at UN summit
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Inspector‑General of Police, Olatunji Disu, has reaffirmed the country’s commitment to strengthening international policing cooperation to promote global peace, security, and sustainable development. He made the remarks at the Fifth United Nations Chiefs of Police Summit in New York. The two‑day sum

item.title: 'Kylian, forgive us': How the World Cup turned Mbappé's reputation around
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: It seems everyone loves Kylian Mbappé again. The France captain, is having a magnificent showing in the World Cup, scoring seven goals so far and breaking records. His performance has led some who previously criticized him for being too outspoken, or for leaving Paris to play in Madrid, to ask for f

item.title: Court remands businessman, three others over assault of mentally challenged teen
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The defendants, Christopher Nnamah, 54, Ndubuisi Ikechelu, 40, Chinecherem Emmanuel, 20, and Kennedy Udogu, 34, at their arraignment on Thursday, had each pleaded not guilty to the charge of rape.

item.description: A joint security operation involving the Zamfara State Police Command and military personnel has rescued a kidnapped a staff of the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, Aliyu Mohammed. item.title: Say hello to Claude Wrapped
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The popularity of Spotify Wrapped has kicked off a wide range of year-in-review features, on apps from YouTube to Uber - and now, the lookback trend has come to AI. Anthropic on Thursday announced a "reflect" feature for its Claude chatbot, allowing users to see an analysis of their usage data over

item.title: Iran launches more strikes after accusing US of striking near nuclear plant
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Centcom says it hit 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes, with the Iranian health ministry saying 14 people were killed since Tuesday.

item.description: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) will begin enforcing the mandatory disclosure of directors’ details on company business letters from August 1, warning that defaulting companies will face sanctions. The commission disclosed this in a public notice issued on Wednesday, stating that the en

item.title: Miyetti Allah president remanded over alleged money laundering, terrorism financing
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abuja on Thursday ordered the remand of the National President of Miyetti Allah Kauta Hore, Bello Bodejo, in the custody of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission, EFCC over an alleged $2.63 million money laundering case. Justice Inyang Ekwo issued the order after Bodej

item.description: By Ben Ngwakwe GOMBE — The Gombe State Government has assured the Christian community of its support following the fire that destroyed the multi-million-naira auditorium of the ECWA Gospel Church, located off Ashaka Road in Gombe metropolis. The fire, which occurred on the night of July 7, 2026, raz

item.title: Mauritania's fishing heritage threatened by climate change and overfishing
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: For centuries, the Imraguen people of Mauritania have relied on wind-powered boats and traditional fishing methods to live in harmony with the rich waters of the Banc d’Arguin. But climate change, overfishing and the gradual loss of ancestral knowledge are threatening both their livelihoods and a un

item.title: The PWHL is Just Getting Started With Hilary Knight
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description:
Hilary Knight is at the height of her ice hockey career, but that doesn't mean she's not already thinking about what she'll do with her time in retirement. On this episode of The Deal, Knight tells Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly why she one day wants to own a team
(Source: Bloomberg)

item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: OGC Nice sporting director Roger Ricort has shared an update on Terem Moffi amid the uncertainty surrounding the Super Eagles striker’s future, Soccernet.ng reports. ​Moffi is among the Nigerian stars expected to switch clubs this summer. He fell out with OGC Nice last November after he and his team

item.title: Wike: 70% of FCT completed projects were long-abandoned contracts of 16 years

item.title: Militia-like movements appear in Chad amid surge in intercommunal violence
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: In Chad, intercommunal violence has dramatically risen, with authorities flagging five different incidents just last month, with over 300 killed in just two years. The UN has warned that violence is escalating across the entire Lake Chad basin, with 5,700 people killed since last November. Violence

item.title: JUST IN: Court Remands Miyetti Allah President In EFCC Custody
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Justice Inyang Ekwo of the Federal High Court, Abuja, has ordered the remand of the National President, Miyetti Allah Kauta Kore, Bello Bodejo, in the custody of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC). On Thursday, Bodejo was arraigned on 12 counts and pleaded not guilty to the charges.

item.title: Tinubu Has No Serious Opposition Ahead of 2027, Says Wike
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, has expressed confidence that President Bola Tinubu faces no formidable opposition ahead of the 2027 general election, arguing that no political party is currently positioned to wrest power from the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC).

item.title: Cloud Security Enters Reality Check as Companies Rethink Accountability
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: For years, cloud adoption was positioned as a fast track to agility, scalability and digital transformation. Today, as organisations operate increasingly distributed environments, many are entering a new phase of cloud maturity; one that requires them to rethink accountability for security across p

item.title: Kaduna Government Applauds NSA for Initiating Forest Guards to Tackle Terrorism, Banditry, Kidnapping
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Linus Aleke in Abuja The Kaduna State Government has applauded the Office of the National Security Adviser (ONSA) for initiating the Forest Guards programme, describing it as a strategic intervention

item.title: Nigeria probes made-up 'presidential' agency that secured thousands in public funds
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Nigeria is investigating a made-up government agency which authorities are saying was cooked up by a conman, and somehow managed to operate under authorities' noses. It had set up offices on government premises and even got itself listed on the budget for allocation of public money. It was supposed

item.title: Nigerian equities surpass South Korea to become top-performing market in dollar terms
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigerian equities have emerged as the best-performing stock market globally in dollar terms this year, overtaking South Korea. The post Nigerian equities surpass South Korea to become top-performing market in dollar terms appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: EU lawmakers call for probe into FIFA’s Infantino
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: EU lawmakers call for probe into FIFA’s Infantino

item.title: Character.AI wants a piece of the microdrama pie
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Character.AI's plan to become more than just an LLM-powered chatbot platform is going beyond interactive books, comics, and audio dramas. Today, the company announced the debut of c.ai Series - short-form, episodic videos designed to be watched and interacted with - on your phone. Unlike traditional

item.title: US Lists 124 Convicted Nigerians For Deportation
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The United States Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has identified 124 Nigerians among foreign nationals slated for deportation as part of an intensified immigration enforcement campaign targeting convicted non-citizens. The updated list was published on the Department’s website on Wednesd

item.title: Natasha urges deployment of digital intelligence to combat terrorism
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Juliet Umeh Senator Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan, representing Kogi Central Senatorial District, has called on Nigeria’s security agencies to deploy digital intelligence and smart technologies to combat terrorism and banditry, arguing that online activities should provide valuable leads for law

item.title: #AriseNewsTownhall: Tracking Funds Crucial to Success of State Police, Says Oshiomhole
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Alex Enumah in Abuja Former Governor of Edo State and Senator representing Edo North at the National Assembly, Senator Adams Oshiomhole, has stressed the need for citizens to track funds

item.title: $4.5bn Fraud Trial: Emefiele suffers setback as court dismisses objections to extra-judicial statements
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Justice Oshodi held that, for an extra-judicial statement to qualify as confessional, it must contain an unequivocal admission of the offences alleged against its maker.

item.title: Gbenga Hashim sues Accord, INEC, urges court to uphold him as party’s presidential candidate
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim has sued Accord Party and INEC, seeking recognition as the party's 2027 presidential candidate. The post Gbenga Hashim sues Accord, INEC, urges court to uphold him as party’s presidential candidate appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.
